title: IDOR and object-level access
description: Checking that a user is authenticated and has a role is not enough; access to a specific object must verify ownership/scope.
body: Use when an endpoint loads a resource by an id from the request. IDOR occurs when access is authorized by role but not by the specific object requested, so a valid user reaches another user's resource by changing an id (horizontal privilege escalation). Do not assume a function-level role check ('is editor') prevents this. Actions: tie the decision to the resource (owner id, tenant id, ACL) at the data layer, and inspect every resource-scoped endpoint for the check. Validation signals include a negative test where a valid principal requests an object they do not own and is denied. Risk boundary: a missing object-level check on user data is a blocking finding.
